comment: Brian (ElevenLabs) VO for Episode 3 (300s): Canada vs Bosnia & Herzegovina, Group B, BMO Field Toronto, June 12 2026. Canon: content/Stories/Canada-vs-Bosnia-and-Herzegovina.md (0-0 draw; 31' Davies cutback / Larin blocked by Vasilj; 2nd half Pjanic free kick / Dzeko header inches wide; shirt swap at FT). Mystery cold open = The Maple Leaf Man (open loop, payoff in Legend segment). Real facts: Davies born in Buduburam refugee camp; Dzeko, 40, grew up during the siege of Sarajevo; Canada 1986 = zero goals; Davies scored Canada's FIRST ever WC goal in 2022; Bosnia's only WC was 2014. `at` = seconds; mux.mjs time-fits lines.

lines.text: Nobody knows his name. But for forty years... at every single Canada match... he has been there. And Toronto whispers: when the Leaf appears... history follows.
Tonight, the Leaf is here.
Last night in Guadalajara, a captain began his goodbye. Tonight, the story moves north — to the cold, to the noise... to Canada.
Welcome back to WorldCup twenty-six Legends, episode three. Canada versus Bosnia. Group B. BMO Field, Toronto.
Fifty thousand Canadians, lighting up the northern night. And on this pitch... two men whose lives should have made football impossible.
Alphonso Davies was born in a refugee camp in Ghana, to parents fleeing war. Tonight he walks out at a home World Cup... as a Champions League winner.
Across the tunnel — Edin Dzeko. Forty years old. He grew up in besieged Sarajevo, learning football between air-raid sirens.
Canada's World Cup history? Nineteen eighty-six: three games, zero goals, goodbye. Then... a thirty-six year wait.
Until Qatar, twenty twenty-two — the kid from the camp rises at the far post... and heads in Canada's FIRST World Cup goal ever. Davies. Of course.
Bosnia has played one World Cup, ever. And these two nations have never met on this stage... until tonight.
So meet the hosts. The Maple Leaf machine of Jesse Marsch — built on pace, pressing, and absolutely no fear.
Alphonso Davies. Twenty-four. The fastest defender on planet Earth — a lightning bolt with a refugee's heart and a champion's medal.
Jonathan David — the silent assassin. He scores goals without ever seeming to try. And Stephen Eustaquio, the metronome from Porto who makes fifty thousand people breathe in rhythm.
Pace. Press. Belief. Canada is not here to make up the numbers. Not this time.
But Bosnia is a miracle that walks. Three and a half million people, a nation born in war — led by its eternal Diamond.
Edin Dzeko. Sixty-five goals for his country. City, Roma, Inter. Forty years old — and this is his very last dance.
Beside him, Miralem Pjanic, whose passes map dimensions others cannot see. And Benjamin Sesko — the young giant who strikes like a hammer.
And there it is — the duel inside the match. The fastest man in football against the wisest. Twenty-four... against forty.
Our storytellers have looked into this Toronto night... and what they saw will give you chills.
Thirty-first minute. Davies receives — and detonates. Kolasinac is two steps behind. Byline. Cutback. LARIN, six yards out —
SAVED! Vasilj! An impossible reflex! BMO Field has its hands on its head!
And then... the second half belongs to the old king. Listen... you can hear fifty thousand hearts slowing down.
Pjanic stands over the free kick. A surgeon's delivery. And Dzeko RISES — the timing has not aged a day —
WIDE! By inches! INCHES! Dzeko looks to the sky... the margins of a whole career, cruel to the very last act.
Full time. Nil-nil. And then, the moment nobody forgets — Davies and Dzeko swap shirts. Youth and age... united in respect.
A point each. Nobody satisfied. Nobody eliminated. Group B is a chess game now — and Switzerland and Qatar are still waiting to move.
Now... remember the beginning? The Leaf. Legend number three: The Maple Leaf Man. Every Canada match since nineteen eighty-six. He never showed his face.
Tonight he watched his team survive. Collect him inside the game.
Which brings us to YOU. At WorldCup twenty-six dot world, pick three of the forty-eight nations — and every goal they score... scores for you.
Free to play. Live leaderboard. Underdogs pay triple. Your picks could change everything.
If this story moved you — like, share it, and subscribe. Next: the United States opens its World Cup... and what we saw is incredible.
